config_under_review: Qwen 3.5 27B Q8 MLX + LoRA + Chen & Truong system prompt (Cell D)
methodology: Each item scored 0-100 on four axes: chassis correctness, logic fidelity, component completeness, biological plausibility. Overall score is the minimum-weighted average — a circuit that fails logic cannot be rescued by clean parts.

items.prompt: CRISPRi-based toggle: two sgRNAs cross-repressing each other's promoters
items.reasoning: sgRNA1 is expressed from p_tet and repression edge targets op_tet -- i.e. sgRNA1 represses its own source (self-repression, not cross-repression). Similarly sgRNA2 from p_lac targets op_lac. Behavior claims mutual cross-repression but the interaction graph implements two independent autorepressed loops. Result is not bistable. Structural rubric missed this because both CDSes have full TU and a cyclic graph exists on paper.

items.prompt: Mammalian toggle switch between two states using TetR and PipR with CMV-driven readouts
items.reasoning: Both regulators (TetR, PipR) AND both reporters (GFP, RFP) are driven by constitutive CMV promoters. The p_tet_off/p_tet_on operators are regulated but nothing transcribes through them -- they dangle. So TetR/PipR repress operators that aren't driving anything, and the reporters are always on independent of state. No bistability. No state-linked readout.

items.prompt: Elowitz repressilator: three mutually repressing repressors (LacI, TetR, CI) with GFP readout
items.reasoning: Cyclic repression topology is right: TetR->p_laci, LacI->p_ci, CI->p_tetr. CI-repressed GFP as monitor is canonical. Naming is non-standard (p_laci means 'promoter driving LacI' which the description clarifies is a tet-repressible promoter) but the description is self-consistent. Would oscillate.

items.prompt: Negative autoregulation of TetR reducing noise in downstream GFP
items.reasoning: p_tet drives TetR which represses p_tet -- canonical negative autoregulation (Rosenfeld/Alon motif). TetR also represses p_tet_out driving GFP. Autoregulation lowers TetR variance, which tightens GFP steady-state -- matches the noise-reduction claim. Clean design.

items.prompt: Beta-carotene production pathway: CrtE, CrtB, CrtI, CrtY expressed from pBAD arabinose-inducible operon
items.reasoning: Enzyme order is biochemically correct: GGPP(CrtE) -> phytoene(CrtB) -> lycopene(CrtI) -> beta-carotene(CrtY). pBAD drives the operon under arabinose -- standard. Simplification: one shared RBS for all four CDSes. Real E. coli operons usually have a dedicated RBS per CDS for balanced translation. Design is structurally simplified but biochemically correct.

items.prompt: CRISPRa activator using dCas9-VP64 with sgRNA recruiting a synthetic activator to upregulate endogenous gene
items.reasoning: Prompt entry was labeled E. coli but VP64 is a mammalian activation domain; model correctly overrode and chose Homo sapiens with CMV + U6 Pol III for sgRNA. The 'activator_rna' hand-waves the SAM/MS2 aptamer recruitment but the concept is real. dCas9-VP64 -> p_target activation edge is correct. Good biological judgment.

items.prompt: Dual-input kill switch in E. coli: without both IPTG and arabinose, CcdB toxin kills cells; both inducers maintain CcdA antitoxin
items.reasoning: Prompt wants NAND survival logic: both inducers -> CcdA > CcdB -> live; missing either -> die. Model put CcdA on pTet (wrong inducer -- pTet responds to aTc, not IPTG) and CcdB on pBAD (arabinose-induced). Without arabinose, CcdB is never expressed, so cells live regardless of IPTG -- the opposite of the required behavior. Model's own behavior text acknowledges the logic is under-specified ('depending on the relative expression balance').

items.prompt: Engineer a tumor-targeting E. coli Nissle circuit: hypoxia-responsive FNR promoter drives InvA; constitutive anti-HER2 nanobody surface-displayed via INP anchor
items.reasoning: pFNR is genuinely hypoxia-activated in E. coli. InvA from Yersinia enables invasion of eukaryotic cells. INP-anchored nanobody surface display is a published technique. Two independent TUs is the right topology. Only minor miss: behavior could mention that InvA under hypoxia localizes invasion to the tumor microenvironment (that's the whole point of the hypoxia sensor) but the parts are all correct.

items.prompt: Yeast galactose utilization rewiring: GAL80 knockout with GAL4-VP64 driving constitutive GAL1/7/10 expression
items.reasoning: GAL80 KO -> GAL4-VP64 always active -> GAL UAS-driven GAL1/7/10 constitutive. Correct mechanism. Stretch: yeast doesn't have bacterial-style operons; GAL1/7/10 share a divergent/adjacent arrangement but each has its own promoter in reality. Representing the three as sharing one p_gal is a useful abstraction but not how yeast actually transcribes them.

items.prompt: CRISPR-Cas13-based RNA sensor: Cas13 cleaves a quenched GFP mRNA when trigger RNA is present
items.reasoning: Prompt describes collateral-cleavage sensing: trigger RNA activates Cas13, which then non-specifically cleaves a quenched reporter (SHERLOCK-style). Model implemented direct targeting: crRNA guides Cas13 to cleave the GFP mRNA itself. There is no trigger RNA in the design, and the mechanism reduces to constitutive GFP-mRNA knockdown rather than trigger-responsive sensing. Topology-correct, biology-wrong.

items.prompt: Cell-free CRISPR sensor: Cas12a activates with trigger DNA, cleaving a quenched fluorescent reporter in a TX-TL reaction
items.reasoning: Concept is right: DETECTR/SHERLOCK-style Cas12a collateral cleavage of a quenched ssDNA reporter. But the reporter is encoded as a 'cds' with RBS and terminator, i.e. the model represents a ssDNA/oligonucleotide probe as if it were a transcribed+translated protein. Cas12a also lacks the 'activation' interaction from trigger DNA (trigger is exogenous, not in the schema, but the activation step isn't captured). Real TX-TL SHERLOCK has no transcription of the reporter.

weak_topologies: CRISPR sensing (collateral vs direct cleavage confusion)

key_finding: The structural rubric averages 91.8 on these same items but the biology rubric averages 71.4. The gap is not noise -- it is a systematic class of failures: the model can produce well-formed transcription units with cyclic graphs while implementing the wrong logic (self-repression masquerading as cross-repression; constitutive reporters in a toggle; NAND that is actually OR). Future work: augment the rubric with a logic-fidelity axis that checks whether the interaction signs actually implement the stated behavior.
